reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim faithfully. The informal "if there is x ∈ G with x^p = a, then the order of x is p²k" is naturally read as the universal "for every x with x^p = a, order(x) = p²k", which is exactly what the Lean signature encodes by fixing x as a parameter with hypothesis hp : x^p = a. All hypotheses match: [Fact p.Prime] corresponds to "p prime" (p is auto-bound as ℕ, matching Nat.Prime), h₀ : k ≥ 1 matches "k ≥ 1", and orderOf a = p * k matches "a has order pk". The conclusion orderOf x = p^2 * k matches "order of x is p²k". The trailing "hence x has larger order than a" is a trivial corollary of the main conclusion (since p ≥ 2 and k ≥ 1 imply p²k > pk) and does not need separate formalization. No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal statement.
